LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. MATTER ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS TO CAUSAL NEXUS AND FIND THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE. WITH REGARD TO PRETEXT, THE COURT BELIEVES THERE COULD BE A TRIABLE ISSUE AS TO WHETHER MR. SWANSON HAD KNOWLEDGE ABOUT MR. JONES' CIRCUMSTANCES. THE PARTIES SHALL SUBMIT FURTHER BRIEFING ON THE ISSUE. DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 9, 2017, AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302. COUNSEL WILL FILE AND SIMULTANEOUSLY EXCHANGE (5-PAGE) BRIEFS ON MARCH 6, 2017, AND PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES TO THE COURT NO LATER THAN MARCH 7, 2017, AT 9:00 A.M. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG (302/HK)
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-01-2017 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY (PG&E)'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. NO APPEARANCES IN THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS PREVIOUS TENTATIVE RULING WITH SOME ADDITIONAL LANGUAGE: SUMMARY JDUGMENT IS DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION AND DENIED AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. COUNSEL FOR MR. KRAMER IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CCP 437C(G) AND ALSO REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-10-2017 Assigned To Trial Court

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-09-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its previous tentative ruling with some additional language. Defendant Pacific Gas and Electric Company (PG&E)'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the first cause of action and denied as to the second and third causes of action. Plaintiff David Kramer acknowledges the lack of merit to his first cause of action for violation of Labor Code 1102.5. (UMF 1.) On the second and third causes of action, there are triable issues whether: a) by his statements at the June 5, 2013 meeting Mr. Kramer engaged in protected activity; b) the corporate security investigation which served as the basis for the decision to terminate Mr. Kramer's employment was biased as a result of Mr. Murman's participation in the investigation; and c) the reasons given for the termination of Mr. Kramer's employment were pretextual because Mr. Jones, who committed the same asserted misconduct as Mr. Kramer, was not terminated even though Mr. Murman had accused Mr. Jones of "falsifying ' time cards." As to protected activity, there is admissible evidence that Mr. Kramer made a complaint to his supervisors about a specific incident related to safety. As to causal nexus, there is admissible evidence that Mr. Murman, who knew of Mr. Kramer's safety complaint, participated in the investigation of Mr. Kramer that Mr. Swanson relied on to terminate Mr. Kramer's employment. As to pretext, there is admissible evidence that Mr. Jones was similarly situated with regard to Mr. Kramer, but for Mr. Kramer's safety complaint, and Mr. Jones was not fired even though he was accused of and committed the same asserted misconduct that was the basis for the termination of Mr. Kramer's employment. At the conclusion of the March 1 hearing the court permitted additional briefing on issue of whether Mr. Swanson's absence of any knowledge of Mr. Jones having been accused of and committed the same asserted misconduct that was the basis for the termination of Mr. Kramer's employment meant that Mr. Kramer is not able to show pretext. The answer to that question is no. Reeves v. Safeway Stores, Inc. (2004) 121 Cal. App. 4th 95 held that where, as here, there is evidence that a lower level supervisor with impermissible animus participated in the chain of events that led to the decision to terminate plaintiff's employment, the animus of the lower level supervisor is imputed to the decisionmaker not only for the element of causation, but also to rebut the employer's proffered legitimate reason for the termination. The after acquired evidence doctrine, even if proved by PG&E, is not a complete bar to either the second or third causes of action, but at most only serve to reduce Mr. Kramer's claimed damages.

Counsel for Mr. Kramer is required to prepare a proposed order that complies with CCP 437c(g) and also repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-01-2017 Continued To Mar-09-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jennifer McGuire, of Hoyer & Hicks, Tel. 415.766.3538, for plaintiff David Kramer;
Joshua D. Kienitz, of Littler Mendelson, P.C., Tel. 415.399.8451, for defendant Pacific Gas and Electric Company;
Katherine Kimsey, of Littler Mendelson, P.C., Tel. 415.677.3168, for defendant Pacific Gas and Electric Company.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as to causal nexus and find there is a triable issue. With regard to pretext, the Court believes there could be a triable issue as to whether Mr. Swanson had knowledge about Mr. Jones' circumstances. The parties shall submit further briefing on the issue. DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is continued to March 9, 2017, at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302. Counsel will file and simultaneously exchange (5-page) briefs on March 6, 2017, and provide courtesy copies to the Court no later than March 7, 2017, at 9:00 a.m. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
